subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Very often, credit card customers are placed on hold when they call for inquiries. Suppose the amount of time until a service agent assists a customer has an exponential distribution with mean 5 minutes. Given that a customer has already been on hold for 2 minutes, what is the probability that he/she will remain on hold for a total of more than 5 minutes?
explanation: Let X represent the total time on hold. Then X is an exponential random variable with $\lambda = \frac{1}{5}$. Thus, $\Pr(X > 3+2 \mid X > 2) = \Pr(X > 3) = 1-F(3) = e^{-3/5}$
